name: Signal Handler Use of a Non-reentrant Function
description: A signal handler calls a non-reentrant function such as malloc(), free(), or syslog(). If the signal interrupts the same function mid-execution, the resulting undefined memory state can lead to memory corruption, crashes, or exploitable write-what-where conditions.
detection_pattern: A STRUCTURAL[signal_handler] node contains a TRANSFORM[function_call] to a non-reentrant EXTERNAL function (malloc, free, syslog, printf) without a CONTROL[async_safe_check] mediator ensuring only async-signal-safe functions are called within the handler.
